mnemonics.title: Energy Drain Sources
mnemonics.content: SCUN - Screen, CPU, United (Network), Nothing (Background services) - remembering major energy consumers in mobile apps

mnemonics.title: Power Management Features
mnemonics.content: DADS - Doze, App Standby, Adaptive Battery, Doze on the go - Android power saving modes in order of introduction

acronyms.usage: Adjusting CPU power based on workload demands
Measuring useful work per unit of energy consumed
Low power CPU state used during idle periods
Framework for analyzing power consumption at application level

visualTips.visualization: Think of power as the speed of a car (how fast energy is used) and energy as the distance traveled (total consumption). Power = Rate, Energy = Total
Imagine a sleeping person who only wakes up briefly at regular intervals to check for important messages, then goes back to sleep - that's Doze mode
Like keeping all lights on in a house even when no one is in the rooms - it prevents the house (device) from going into power-saving mode
Like turning off the main power switch to unused rooms in a house to prevent phantom power consumption

analogies.analogy: Energy profiling is like using a fuel consumption meter in a car to identify which driving habits or car components use the most fuel
Battery capacity is like a fuel tank - energy analysis helps you understand how your app 'drives' to consume this limited fuel efficiently
Network batching is like combining multiple errands into one trip instead of making separate trips for each, saving fuel (energy)
Background services are like appliances left running in standby mode - they don't do much but still consume power over time
